subject: HDA: Online Enrollment Schedule for Term 1 AY 2026-2027
body: The Office of the University Registrar announces that online enrollment for Term 1 AY 2026-2027 will run from July 10 to July 15, 2026. Undergraduate students must complete course enlistment and confirmation of enrollment through Animo.sys before 11:59 PM on July 15, 2026. Students with unpaid balances should coordinate with the Accounting Office before enrollment confirmation.

subject: HDA: Tuition Payment Deadline and Installment Reminders
body: The Accounting Office reminds students that the first installment for Term 1 tuition must be settled on or before July 20, 2026. Payments may be made through accredited banks and online channels. Students with payment concerns should file a ticket with the Help Desk before the deadline to avoid enrollment restrictions.

subject: HDA: Temporary Gate Access Procedure for External Guests
body: The Campus Safety and Mobility Office will implement a temporary gate access procedure from July 8 to July 31, 2026. Faculty members and student organizations inviting external guests must submit the guest list, purpose of visit, and host contact details at least three working days before the scheduled visit. Guests must present a valid government-issued ID at the gate.

subject: HDA: CCS Thesis Proposal Defense Submission
body: The College of Computer Studies announces that thesis proposal defense documents for eligible undergraduate groups must be submitted to the department secretary by July 18, 2026. Required files include the proposal manuscript, adviser endorsement, and ethics checklist. Late submissions will be scheduled for the next available defense cycle.

subject: HDA: Campus Health Advisory for Flu-Like Symptoms
body: The Health Services Office advises members of the Lasallian community to stay home when experiencing fever, persistent cough, or flu-like symptoms. Students who miss graded activities due to illness should secure a medical certificate and coordinate with their instructors. Mask use is recommended in crowded indoor areas.

subject: HDA: Scheduled AnimoSpace Maintenance
body: Information Technology Services will conduct scheduled AnimoSpace maintenance on July 12, 2026 from 12:00 AM to 6:00 AM. During this period, users may experience intermittent access to course pages, quizzes, and uploaded files. Faculty members are encouraged to avoid setting online deadlines during the maintenance window.

subject: HDA: Student Organization Renewal Requirements
body: The Student Activities Office reminds recognized student organizations to submit renewal documents by August 2, 2026. Requirements include the updated officer roster, adviser conforme, constitution and bylaws, and accomplishment report for the previous academic year.

subject: Limited Time Laptop Sale
body: Buy discounted accessories and laptops today. This is a promotional message unrelated to university announcements.

subject: Dinner later?
body: Are you free after class? Let's decide where to eat near campus.

subject: [OVPERI] Kanazawa University Student Exchange Program Spring 2027
body:
Office of the Vice President for External Relations and Internationalization
🖂 ovperi.exchange@dlsu.edu.ph
 
The Office of the Vice President for External Relations and Internationalization
 
invites undergraduate and graduate students to apply to
 
Kanazawa University, Japan
Student Exchange Program for Spring 2027
 
Kanazawa University, one of Japan’s National Universities, can trace its history back to the opening of a small medical school in 1862. Originally a regional smallpox vaccination clinic, the school has grown over its 150-plus year history to become a leading centre for higher education.
 
The university campus lies in green hills to the east of the coastal city of Kanazawa, built on the shores of the Sea of Japan. The university hospital has its own campus in the city centre.
 
Kanazawa University has reorganised its faculties into three colleges with 16 schools in-keeping with its aim of being "a research university dedicated to education, while opening up its doors to both local and global society".
 
Kanazawa is an ancient castle city with a long history of education. The city is surrounded by the Japanese Alps and experiences heavy snows in winter perfect for skiing and snowboarding. There are also two nearby national parks and two rivers flow through the centre of Kanazawa.
 
Its beautiful location (only two-and-a-half hours from Tokyo by train) means there has always been a community of artists and craftsmen working in Kanazawa. Ancient castles and temples blend with the city’s modern infrastructure to create a charming place to study.
 
The university has many specialist institutes including the Advanced Science Research Centre, the University Hospital (which dates from the beginning of the university in 1862), the Institute for Nature and Environmental Technology and the Centre for Archaeological Research. Kanazawa welcomes foreign students and partners with many international institutions across the world. [source: THE]
 
Exchange period: April 2027 - September 2027
 
List of study fields
School of Humanities
School of Law
School of Economics
School of Teacher Education
School of Regional Development Studies
School of International Studies
 
Explore the syllabus here:

Credits
1 credit is equivalent to 1.5-2 ECTS.
 
Tuition
Tuition fees at Kanazawa University are waived under the exchange agreement. Limited slots available.
 
Internal Requirements
Nomination: Students must be nominated by DLSU ℅ OVPERI
Prepare the documentary requirements below:
Outbound Exchange Program Application Form
Copy of grades
Copy of curriculum audit
Copy of the EAF (current term)
Recommendation letter
Application letter
Passport bio-page
Latest resume
Eligibility:
Must have completed at least one (1) academic year in DLSU;
Must not be a graduating student;
Must be enrolled at the time of the program;
Must have a cumulative grade point average of 3.0;
Must have no failing grade;
Must have not committed any major offense as determined by SDFO;
Proficient in English or other language that may be required by the host University;
Must have good potential to succeed in an international academic environment;
Financially capable to assume expenses for round-trip transportation and other travel related costs, insurance, books and school supplies, and other personal and living expenses.
For interested students, kindly follow the steps below:
 
Complete this form: https://forms.gle/9BrNW3AANHNzvd1n9 not later than July 13, 2026. Ensure that you use your official DLSU email address when filling out our registration form; personal email addresses will not be accepted. Those who fail to register by the given deadline will not receive the application instructions, and their applications will not be acknowledged.
Wait for the email from ovperi.exchange@dlsu.edu.ph with detailed instructions on how to apply, together with the program fact sheet.
Please note that the OVPERI Application Deadline is on July 27, 2026.
 
Thank you.
